title: Arrogance and Pride (Kibr / Takabbur)
subtitle: The Trait That Expelled Iblees from Jannah

verses.arabic: وَلَا تُصَعِّرْ خَدَّكَ لِلنَّاسِ وَلَا تَمْشِ فِي الْأَرْضِ مَرَحًا ۖ إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يُحِبُّ كُلَّ مُخْتَالٍ فَخُورٍ
verses.translation: And do not turn your cheek [in contempt] toward people and do not walk through the earth exultantly. Indeed, Allah does not like everyone self-deluded and boastful.

verses.arabic: قَالَ فَاهْبِطْ مِنْهَا فَمَا يَكُونُ لَكَ أَن تَتَكَبَّرَ فِيهَا فَاخْرُجْ إِنَّكَ مِنَ الصَّاغِرِينَ
verses.translation: [Allah] said, 'Descend from it, for it is not for you to be arrogant therein. So get out; indeed, you are of the debased.'

verses.arabic: قِيلَ ادْخُلُوا أَبْوَابَ جَهَنَّمَ خَالِدِينَ فِيهَا ۖ فَبِئْسَ مَثْوَى الْمُتَكَبِّرِينَ
verses.translation: It will be said, 'Enter the gates of Hell to abide eternally therein, and wretched is the residence of the arrogant.'

brief_explanation: Kibr (arrogance) was the first sin committed in creation — by Iblees, who refused to prostrate to Adam out of pride, saying 'I am better than him.' The Quran records this as the cause of his eternal expulsion. Allah declares that He does not love the self-deluded and boastful (mukhtal, fakhur) and that the abode of the mutakabbireen (the arrogant) is the gates of Hell, to dwell therein eternally. The Prophet (peace be upon him) defined kibr as 'rejecting the truth and looking down upon people' — making it an internal disease that distorts one's relationship with both Allah and creation. Even a mustard seed's weight of kibr in the heart prevents entry into Jannah.
capital_punishment: There is no hadd (fixed corporal punishment) prescribed for kibr. It is a sin of the heart addressed through tawbah, self-accounting (muhasabah), and cultivating humility (tawadu'). If arrogance manifests in oppressing others, those specific acts of oppression carry their own rulings. The ultimate punishment for dying upon kibr is stated clearly in the Quran: the gates of Hell, for eternity.
